Me, explaining human culture to my cat: “… so to us, it is the height of disrespect to take something someone has given you as a gift and gently knock it to the ground and stare them in the eyes”

@quinnnorton I can totally understand why you wouldnt want to repeat that! There is no right way. Maybe try to sandwich between activities you know you enjoy. No professional would suggest it, but I would meditate before my morning cigarette and reward myself with it afterwards.
@quinnnorton I also think its totally okay to sit for a minute or two and be done! You did it! Thats success, not failure. The sitting is the doing.

@quinnnorton Thats why I suggest a visualization technique, you arent leaving room for nothingness, focusing on imagery instead of “thinking”. Filling with warm light is good. I also like to imagine my head is filled with water crashing back and forth, and I focus on making the water flat.

@quinnnorton Finally, it is hard for me to see meditation as “useful”- I have to reframe it as “I am doing this exercise for the people around me” - I know I treat others around me better when I have taken the time to assess and calm my mind. To me its like putting on deoderant.

@quinnnorton I also had a lot of trouble in the beginning with “see a thought, breathe, move on” and gravitated more towards visualization techniques like imagining golden liquid slowly filling your body with good, calm energy as you scan from toes to crown of head- something to focus on.
